market_summary: US equities closed sharply lower on Friday, with the Nasdaq Composite falling over 2% and the S&P 500 losing 1.2%, as a global semiconductor selloff intensified following a Chinese AI breakthrough. The Dow Jones Industrial Average shed 406 points, pressured by a spike in crude oil prices amid escalating Middle East tensions. Defensive and energy sectors outperformed, while technology and growth stocks bore the brunt of the rotation.
macro_summary: Stagflationary Pulse with AI Leadership Uncertainty The market is in a risk-off rotation driven by a dual shock: a challenge to US AI leadership and a geopolitical oil spike. Investors are moving from growth to value, from tech to energy, and from large-cap to small-cap. Today's market action reflects a regime where growth stocks are being repriced on AI competition fears while energy costs rise on geopolitical risk, creating a stagflationary pulse. The market is caught between a resilient economy and sticky inflation, with the Fed on hold and the consumer showing signs of strain. The macro backdrop is increasingly challenged by a confluence of geopolitical risk, rising energy costs, and a potential reset in AI leadership assumptions. The CNBC All-America Economic Survey showed deepening consumer pessimism, with the public blaming Trump for the worsening outlook. Homebuyer affordability slipped for a fifth straight month, while oil supply disruptions threaten to add to inflationary pressures. The market is pricing in a higher probability of a 'no landing' scenario where growth slows but inflation remains sticky.
risk_summary: Risk appetite collapsed today, with the VIX surging to 22.5, its highest level in a month. Breadth was decisively negative, with decliners outpacing advancers 3:1 on the NYSE. The put/call ratio spiked to 1.15, indicating defensive positioning. US equity funds saw outflows for the week, with tech and semiconductor ETFs hit hardest. The move was orderly but lacked panic, suggesting investors are repositioning rather than capitulating. The energy sector was the only bright spot, with the XLE up 1.5%.

top_events.title: Iran Conflict Escalation: Oil Tanker Attacks and US Strikes
top_events.summary: Iran attacked oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, killing one seafarer, while the US conducted strikes on civilian infrastructure in Iran, Syria, and Bahrain. The fragile truce signed last month showed signs of unravelling, pushing crude oil above $85/barrel.

top_events.title: Apple tops Nvidia as world's most valuable company as AI bets shift
top_events.summary: Apple has surpassed Nvidia to become the world's most valuable company, driven by a shift in AI investment focus away from pure-play semiconductor makers toward consumer-facing AI integration.

top_events.title: China AI Breakthrough: Moonshot AI Kimi K3 Rattles US Tech
top_events.summary: Moonshot AI's new open-weight model, Kimi K3, was reported to close the performance gap with leading US AI labs, triggering a global semiconductor selloff. The market interpreted this as a threat to US AI dominance and a potential commoditization of AI infrastructure.

top_events.title: Wall St ends lower for the day and week as chip selloff broadens
top_events.summary: U.S. stock markets closed lower for both the session and the week, driven by a widening selloff in semiconductor stocks.

top_events.title: Trump wants rate cuts. More Fed officials say hikes could be next.
top_events.summary: Political pressure for monetary easing is mounting from the White House, but an increasing number of Federal Reserve officials are signaling that rate hikes may be necessary.

top_events.title: Consumer Sentiment Hits Post-Pandemic Lows, Trump Blamed
top_events.summary: The CNBC All-America Economic Survey showed the public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic, with Trump getting blamed for the worsening outlook.

top_events.title: Netflix Q2 Earnings Miss on Subscriber Growth, Weak Q3 Forecast
top_events.summary: Netflix reported Q2 earnings that beat on revenue but missed on subscriber growth and issued a weak Q3 forecast. The company also announced it would reduce the frequency of engagement reports, raising concerns about transparency and growth sustainability.

top_events.title: White House to Control Access to Frontier AI Models
top_events.summary: The Trump administration is taking steps to control who gets access to the latest frontier AI models, shifting power from tech giants. This adds regulatory uncertainty to the AI trade and could impact companies like Anthropic and OpenAI.

sector_summary: Positive sectors/themes: Energy, Defense, Utilities, Consumer Staples; Negative sectors/themes: Semiconductors, Technology Hardware, Growth Software, Consumer Discretionary; Themes to watch: AI commoditization and the shift from infrastructure to application layer, Geopolitical risk premium in energy and defense, Value vs. growth rotation as rates remain elevated, Earnings season divergence between AI winners and laggards
